homepage.text_excerpt: Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP – MP for Maldon Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Working for the people of Maldon locally, and in Westminster. Working for the people of Maldon locally, and in Westminster. Working for the people of Maldon locally, and in Westminster. Working for the people of Maldon locally, and in Westminster. Welcome to my website As Member of Parliament for Maldon, I am committed to representing residents locally and speaking up for our area in Westminster. Here you can find updates on my work in Parliament and across the constituency, including campaigns, visits, and the issues raised with me by residents. My priority is to listen, act where I can, and ensure that local voices are heard at the heart of Government. The Maldon Mud Race John Whittingdale on Peter Mandelson New Health Centre Funding Charity Bet Campaign in Coral, Maldon New District Commander for Chelmsford and Maldon Churchill’s Fish and Chips Campaign Responses I receive a growing number of emails from constituents about national and local campaigns. I try to respond to as many people as possible, as quickly as I can. Where a large number of residents have contacted me about the same issue, I will also publish my response here so that everyone can see my position and the action I am taking. If you have written to me, please include your full name and postcode so that I can confirm you are a constituent and ensure your message is handled appropriately. Latest News from Westminster & the Constituency The Maldon Mud Race Raising £30,000 for local charities, The Maldon Mud race took place recently. John Whittingdale on Peter Mandelson Sir John Whittingdale cites committee evidence that proper vetting was bypassed, highlights Downing Street’s role in forcing Mandelson's appointment, and warns of national security risks and potential damage to UK–US relations. Charity Bet Campaign in Coral, Maldon Sir John Whittingdale visits Coral to support the Betting and Gaming Council Charity Bet Campaign, to help raise funds for local carers. New District Commander for Chelmsford and Maldon Sir John met new District Commander Chief Inspector Aaron Homatopoulos, hearing about local policing and the broader work Essex Police do in the community. Churchill’s Fish and Chips With HospitalityUK, Sir John visits Churchill’s Fish and Chips, a four-generation local family business since 1923, seeing firsthand the challenges high street restaurants face. South Woodham Ferrers Men’s Shed Sir John visits South Woodham Ferrers Men’s Shed, meeting Chair John Bevan to discuss new premises and fundraising, learning firsthand about the organisation’s work to foster friendship, creativity, and community spirit. Newsletter Sign-Up If you would like to receive occasional updates from Westminster and across the constituency, you can sign up to my newsletter using the link below. I share news, local visits, campaigns, and key parliamentary work—so you can stay informed about what I am doing on your behalf. Sign up to my newsletter Requests & Visits Work Experience Requests I receive a large number of requests for work experience placements each year. Opportunities are offered where possible to local students and residents to provide an insight into the work of a Member of Parliament and the operation of both the Westminster and constituency offices. If you would like to be considered, please contact my office with details of your availability, interests and a short summary of why you would like to take part. Application Visits to Westminster Tickets for Prime Minister’s Questions are extremely limited and demand is high, so I operate a waiting list for constituents who would like to attend. Victoria Tower tours, which include access to the Elizabeth Tower and Big Ben, are also very restr

pages.text_excerpt: Contact John – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Contact John You can contact Sir John Whittingdale by telephone, email, or by writing to him. If you would like to speak to John in person, he holds regular surgeries in the constituency. Post Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Phone Westminster: 0207 219 3557 Surgery bookings: 01621 855663 Email To contact John via email please use Write to Them. Alternatively send to: john.whittingdale.mp@parliament.uk How John can help Sir John and his team of caseworkers assist constituents by contacting local authorities, government departments and relevant organisations on their behalf to raise issues. What an MP cannot assist with Legal matters, court decisions or private disputes; financial or legal advice; and most local planning decisions, which sit with the local planning authority. MPs also do not control local council decisions, although issues can be raised with the Council. Please note When contacting John, include your full address. If writing on behalf of someone else, written consent will be required for GDPR purposes. If requesting assistance with a case, include any relevant reference numbers. Who else can help? Local councillors are often best placed to assist with issues such as school places or waste collection. Citizens Advice provides free and impartial guidance on benefits, housing, debt and employment. The Maldon and District branch can be contacted on 01621 875774. Advice surgeries John continues to hold advice surgeries and encourages constituents to attend if they would like to raise an issue. To book, call 01621 855663 or email office@maldonconservatives.org. Members of Parliament can only assist their own constituents. You can check if Sir John is your MP using the Find Your MP page on the UK Parliament website. Published by Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP — Member of Parliament for Maldon 19 High Street, Maldon, Essex, CM9 5PE Westminster office: 020 7219 3557 john.whittingdale.mp@parliament.uk © John Whittingdale MP 2026 Privacy · Cookies · Accessibility We use cookies on our website to give you the most relevant experience by remembering your preferences and repeat visits. pages.title: Local News – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
headings: h2: Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
h2: Member of Parliament for Maldon
pages.text_excerpt: Local News – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Local News & Constituency Visits This page brings together local news and constituency updates from Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP, including visits to schools, businesses, charities, community groups and local events. It is a record of ongoing work across the constituency—listening to residents, supporting local priorities, and highlighting the organisations and people who make our area thrive. The Maldon Mud Race Raising £30,000 for local charities, The Maldon Mud race took place recently. Charity Bet Campaign in Coral, Maldon Sir John Whittingdale visits Coral to support the Betting and Gaming Council Charity Bet Campaign, to help raise funds for local carers. New District Commander for Chelmsford and Maldon Sir John met new District Commander Chief Inspector Aaron Homatopoulos, hearing about local policing and the broader work Essex Police do in the community. Churchill’s Fish and Chips With HospitalityUK, Sir John visits Churchill’s Fish and Chips, a four-generation local family business since 1923, seeing firsthand the challenges high street restaurants face. South Woodham Ferrers Men’s Shed Sir John visits South Woodham Ferrers Men’s Shed, meeting Chair John Bevan to discuss new premises and fundraising, learning firsthand about the organisation’s work to foster friendship, creativity, and community spirit. Heybridge Flood Alleviation Scheme Sir John Whittingdale celebrates the Environment Agency’s £507,000 investment in the Heybridge Flood Alleviation Scheme, noting it as an important step to address persistent flooding concerns for local residents. Call of the Wild Zoo Visit to Call of the Wild Zoo to mark English Tourism Week. Sir John met with the Zoo Directors to discuss their work and some of the issues they face. Maldon & Southminster NFU Sir John met members of the National Farmers' Union in Maldon and Southminster to discuss key rural issues and reaffirmed his commitment to raising farmers’ concerns in Parliament. Morrisons’ Holiday Hunger Initiative Sir John visited Morrisons to learn about the Kellogg’s & Morrisons Free Breakfast Holiday Hunger Initiative, meeting staff and highlighting the scheme’s support for families during school holidays. BBC Any Questions? Sir John appeared on the BBC Any Questions? panel, discussing issues such as the Conflict in Iran, the Special Relationship, energy security, artificial intelligence and economic growth in the UK. Maldon Banking Hub Sir John welcomes plans for a temporary banking hub in Maldon, improving access to cash and basic services, while continuing to press for a permanent, centrally located High Street facility. Asda Grants Sir John welcomes Asda’s local community support, with grants awarded to South Woodham Evangelical Church and South Woodham Ferrers Village Hall, and encourages organisations to apply for future funding opportunities through 2026. Page 1 Page 2 Page 3 Page 4 Page 5 Published by Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP — Member of Parliament for Maldon 19 High Street, Maldon, Essex, CM9 5PE Westminster office: 020 7219 3557 john.whittingdale.mp@parliament.uk © John Whittingdale MP 2026 Privacy · Cookies · Accessibility We use cookies on our website to give you the most relevant experience by remembering your preferences and repeat visits. pages.title: Speeches – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
pages.h1: House of Commons Speeches & Parliamentary Questions
headings: h2: Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
h2: Member of Parliament for Maldon
pages.text_excerpt: Speeches – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About House of Commons Speeches & Parliamentary Questions This page brings together speeches, statements, and parliamentary questions delivered in the House of Commons by Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP. Entries are presented chronologically and include both oral contributions in the Chamber and written questions submitted as part of the formal parliamentary process. John Whittingdale on Peter Mandelson Sir John Whittingdale cites committee evidence that proper vetting was bypassed, highlights Downing Street’s role in forcing Mandelson's appointment, and warns of national security risks and potential damage to UK–US relations. New Health Centre Funding Sir John Whittingdale presses the Government to include Maldon in future health centre funding rounds and requests a meeting to advance plans for a long-promised local health hub. Local Government Reorganisation Sir John Whittingdale questions feasibility of rapid Essex reorganisation, warning pressures from elections, NHS changes, and social care demand make the Government’s timetable unmanageable and urges reconsideration. Support for Media Freedom Sir John Whittingdale welcomes the UK co-chairing the Media Freedom Coalition and urges financial support for independent media where press freedom is declining, noting reduced US backing. UK Response to Iran Parliamentary question to the Prime Minister, Keir Starmer, about his response to the situation in Iran, asking for clarity on his lack of support for the actions of UK allies. UK Media Freedom Parliamentary Question challenging the Chief Secretary about media freedom, and the UK's upcoming role as chair of the global Media Freedom Coalition, in light of allegations against Labour Together. Foreign Office Budget Cuts Sir John argued against cuts to the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office budget, stressing the need to protect soft power institutions like the BBC World Service and British Council while supporting defence funding. Lord Mandelson Concerns raised in Parliament over Lord Mandelson’s appointment, potential conflicts of interest, and transparency around Global Counsel shareholding, with calls for disclosure and scrutiny of safeguards. Journalist Safety in Iran Urgent Question in Parliament on Iran’s crackdown on protesters, with calls for accountability, sanctions, and support for journalists and investigators to establish responsibility and pursue justice. China and Japan Parliamentary exchange on the Prime Minister’s visit to China and Japan, including concerns raised over China’s role in supplying Russia and implications for UK security and foreign policy. Russian-Occupied Territories Parliamentary debate on Ukraine highlighting Russia’s aggression, humanitarian conditions in occupied territories, and continued calls for sustained international support and accountability for violations of international law. Support for Local Businesses Parliamentary question highlighting concerns from Burnham-on-Crouch businesses over rising business rates, seeking assurance that forthcoming reforms will support small high street firms facing financial pressure. Page 1 Page 2 Page 3 Page 4 Page 5 Published by Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP — Member of Parliament for Maldon 19 High Street, Maldon, Essex, CM9 5PE Westminster office: 020 7219 3557 john.whittingdale.mp@parliament.uk © John Whittingdale MP 2026 Privacy · Cookies · Accessibility We use cookies on our website to give you the most relevant experience by remembering your preferences and repeat visits. pages.title: Campaigns – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
pages.meta_description: Sir John opposes any mandatory digital ID system, supporting voluntary tools only where offline alternatives remain available, and pledges to defend privacy, liberty and personal choice as government policy develops.
headings: h2: Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP
h2: Member of Parliament for Maldon
pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Campaigns Many residents contact me through organised email campaigns on national and local issues. These messages are an important way for constituents to make their views known, and I carefully consider the points raised when responding and when representing our area in Parliament. Where a large number of people write to me about the same campaign, I will often publish my response here so that residents can see my position and the action I am taking. This helps ensure transparency, keeps constituents informed, and allows everyone to understand how I am engaging with issues raised by the community. Labelling Reforms Sir John supports clearer animal welfare food labelling, calling for greater transparency so consumers can make informed choices and British farmers are not undercut by lower-standard imports. Public Order Act Sir John supports peaceful protest but says any Public Order Act changes affecting life sciences must be justified, balanced and protect both animal welfare standards and the UK’s ability to deliver vital medical research. WASPI Campaign Sir John urges the Government to bring clarity and a fair resolution to the WASPI compensation review, stressing the need for trust, accountability and timely decisions for women affected. Social Media Ban Sir John recognises growing harms from social media use among children, supports stronger enforcement of existing laws, and says technology companies must be held accountable before considering a statutory ban for under-16s. Nuclear Regulatory Review Sir John says energy infrastructure and nature protection must go hand in hand, and will scrutinise the Nuclear Regulatory Review to ensure reforms do not weaken environmental safeguards or commitments to wildlife and habitats. Digital ID Sir John opposes mandatory digital ID, backing voluntary tools only with offline alternatives and warning compulsory systems risk undermining privacy, liberty and public trust. Fireworks and Animal Welfare Response to constituents’ concerns about fireworks, recognising the impact on animals and vulnerable people and supporting consideration of quieter, more responsible use while maintaining traditional celebrations. Planning and Infrastructure Bill Response to constituents’ concerns about the Planning and Infrastructure Bill, stressing the need to protect nature, maintain local accountability and ensure reforms do not weaken environmental safeguards. Agricultural Property Relief Response to constituents’ concerns about changes to Agricultural and Business Property Relief, warning of the impact on family farms and rural communities and urging the Government to reconsider. Digital ID Response to constituents’ concerns about digital identity proposals, supporting voluntary systems to improve services while stressing the importance of privacy, personal liberty and strong data protections. Firearms Legislation Proposals to align shotgun legislation with wider firearms controls, stressing the importance of public safety while protecting legitimate rural and farming uses. Chagos Islands Response to constituents’ concerns about proposals to transfer sovereignty of the Chagos Islands, raising security, financial and strategic implications and pressing the Government for clarity and scrutiny. Page 1 Page 2 Page 3 Page 4 Page 5 Published by Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP — Member of Parliament for Maldon 19 High Street, Maldon, Essex, CM9 5PE Westminster office: 020 7219 3557 john.whittingdale.mp@parliament.uk © John Whittingdale MP 2026 Privacy · Cookies · Accessibility We use cookies on our website to give you the most relevant experience by remembering your preferences and repeat visits. pages.text_excerpt: About – Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Skip to content Rt Hon Sir John Whittingdale OBE MP Member of Parliament for Maldon Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About Home Contact John Local News Westminster News Speeches Campaigns About About Sir John Whittingdale serves as the Member of Parliament for Maldon, representing residents in Westminster and working on their behalf across a wide range of national and local issues. His role is to represent constituents, scrutinise legislation, contribute to national policy, and ensure that the interests of Maldon and its communities are heard in Parliament and Government. This section provides further information about Sir John’s background, the constituency he represents, and the role of a Member of Parliament. About Sir John Sir John Whittingdale’s parliamentary career, ministerial service and experience in public life. Read more About Maldon Information on the towns, villages and communities represented, and the character and heritage of the constituency. Read more The role of an MP An overview of what Members of Parliament do, how they support constituents, and the responsibilities of parliamentary representation. Read more Supporting constituents A central part of Sir John’s work is assisting residents with issues affecting their daily lives — from navigating government services and raising concerns with public bodies, to representing local matters at national level. Members of Parliament act on behalf of all constituents, regardless of how they voted. Get in touch If you are a constituent and require assistance, or would like to raise an issue, please contact Sir John’s office .
